data.title: SONEX Rises 15% as AI Integration Boosts DeFi Efficiency
data.description:
The convergence of artificial intelligence (AI) and decentralized finance (DeFi) is revolutionizing the crypto landscape, addressing critical challenges that have hindered the growth of DeFi applications. AI promises to enhance the efficiency and ease of use for DeFi users by tackling issues such as liquidity fragmentation, simplifying complex cross-chain interactions, and automating yield farming strategies. This integration can automate complex transaction flows, optimize swap routes, and analyze market conditions, potentially reducing the technical barriers to entry for DeFi users.

While this technology is still in its early stages and somewhat experimental, the crypto community's willingness to adopt cutting-edge solutions has led to significant progress. Several projects are at the forefront of this innovation, leveraging AI to transform the DeFi experience. One such project is SONEX, the top DeFi protocol on Sony’s Soneium blockchain. SONEX aims to address liquidity fragmentation and automate trading strategies for users. Its all-in-one DeFi hub combines AI-based trading insights with smart routing capabilities, helping users identify the most profitable token swap routes across any blockchain ecosystem. SONEX's Multi-Chain Bridge facilitates the flow of cross-chain funds, enabling any protocol to connect to the Soneium network. Additionally, SONEX offers AI agents that help traders identify efficient trading strategies by sourcing liquidity from across any network. The project's "Alpha Hunting" feature employs AI agents to discover profitable trades by analyzing market prices in real time, providing users with an edge over other traders.

Another notable project is THENA, which positions itself as the BNB Chain ecosystem’s “liquidity layer.” THENA aims to streamline crypto trading experiences by providing better incentives to liquidity providers and token holders. Its key update, THENA ve(3,3), aligns the incentives of traders, veTHE token holders, liquidity providers, and DeFi protocols through an AI-powered dynamic. This ensures that the emission rate of its native THE token, the “bribes” paid by protocols, and the rewards paid to liquidity providers are determined by market mechanics. THENA offers various strategies for protocols to incentivize liquidity, including acquiring veTHE tokens, deploying their own liquidity, and using bribe deposits to increase THE emissions. These mechanisms ensure that every stakeholder is equally incentivized, based on the concept of “decentralized emissions.”

data.title: Cardano (ADA) Drops 28% Amid Altcoin Panic, Key Support Zone Holds
data.description:
Cardano (ADA) has experienced significant volatility and price swings, with the cryptocurrency market facing widespread panic as altcoins continue to struggle. Over the past few days, ADA has dropped more than 28% since last Thursday, contributing to the growing negative sentiment surrounding altcoins. As uncertainty grips the market, investors are closely monitoring whether Cardano can stabilize or if further declines are imminent.

Despite the bearish sentiment, there are indications that Cardano could be poised for a recovery rally. Top analyst Ali Martinez shared a technical analysis revealing that ADA’s key support zone lies between $0.80 and $0.66. Holding above this level has been crucial for setting up a market rebound. If ADA can maintain support within this range, it could pave the way for a strong recovery, potentially leading to a renewed push toward higher resistance levels.

With market conditions remaining fragile, the coming days will be pivotal for Cardano’s next move. Traders are watching whether ADA can hold key levels or if continued selling pressure will drive prices lower. A successful defense of support could mark the beginning of a recovery, but failure to hold may signal further downside risk.

Cardano has shown resilience compared to the broader market over the past week, holding key support levels despite ongoing selling pressure. While many altcoins have suffered significant declines, ADA has remained relatively stable. However, price action is still confined to a range that first began forming in November 2024, preventing a clear breakout in either direction.

If bulls can defend the current levels, ADA could soon attempt a push above multi-year highs, potentially leading to a strong recovery rally. Despite its recent stability, analysts remain cautious, noting that prices remain low and sentiment suggests that many investors are still fearful. The next few weeks will be crucial in determining whether Cardano can sustain its momentum or if further downside is ahead.

Martinez’s technical analysis highlights that, when looking at the bigger picture, Cardano’s key support zone lies between $0.80 and $0.66. He noted that holding above this critical range would be essential in determining ADA’s next major move. If ADA remains within this zone and starts to gain momentum, it could set the stage for a significant breakout, fueling a move toward higher resistance levels.

Strategy Adds 246,876 BTC, Faces $3B Paper Loss
data.description:
Strategy, a prominent corporate entity, has amassed an extensive Bitcoin reserve, holding 499,096 BTC valued at approximately $40.9 billion. The company's average purchase price for these Bitcoins stands at $66,357 per coin. Since November 2024, Strategy has aggressively expanded its holdings, acquiring 246,876 BTC at an average price of $94,035 per coin, resulting in a total expenditure of $23.2 billion. However, due to the volatility of Bitcoin's market value, these newly acquired assets are now worth $20.2 billion, leading to a paper loss of around $3 billion.

The most significant period of acquisition occurred between November 18 and November 24, 2024, when Strategy purchased 55,500 BTC at an average price of $97,862 per coin. This acquisition brought the company's total Bitcoin reserves to 386,700 BTC. Despite the recent downturn in Bitcoin's market value, Strategy has continued to add to its reserves, reflecting its long-term commitment to cryptocurrency. The company's largest acquisition occurred between February 18 and 23, 2025, when it added 20,356 BTC at an average price of $97,514.

Strategy's financial position has been further strengthened by a $2 billion convertible notes offering, which allowed the company to add 20,356 BTC to its holdings for approximately $1.99 billion. This purchase has bolstered the company's overall acquisition in 2025, generating a 6.9% BTC yield year-to-date. As of February 23, 2025, Strategy has accumulated 499,096 BTC, acquired for around $33.1 billion at an average price of $66,357 per Bitcoin. Despite the recent slowdown in acquisitions, the company remains one of the most significant corporate holders of Bitcoin.

Strategy's unwavering commitment to Bitcoin is evident in its continued acquisitions despite market fluctuations. The company's average purchase price of $66,357 per Bitcoin highlights its long-term strategy, which involves accumulating Bitcoin regardless of short-term price movements.

data.title: AI Agents Crypto Sector Plummets 77.5% From Peak, Solana’s AI Agents Drop 4.3%
data.description:
The artificial intelligence (AI) agents crypto sector has experienced a significant downturn, with its total market capitalization falling below $5 billion. This decline has sparked concerns about the sector's stability and its long-term potential. The downturn follows a period of rapid growth, where the majority of top AI agent tokens saw substantial gains, peaking between December and January. However, this momentum has since reversed, with nearly all AI tokens following a similar trajectory in the crypto market.

The sector has declined by 77.5% from its peak, with a 6.8% loss in the past 24 hours alone, bringing its total market capitalization down to $4.4 billion. All of the top 10 AI tokens have recorded double-digit losses over the past week, indicating a widespread correction. The downturn is spread across multiple blockchains, with Solana’s AI Agents sector seeing a 4.3% decline over the past day, and its market capitalization standing at $1.1 billion. Similarly, Base’s AI sector has dropped to $736.6 million, marking a 5.8% loss over the same period. Other blockchain networks hosting AI-related tokens have been hit even harder, with their collective market cap shrinking to $722.2 million, down a staggering 15.2% in the last 24 hours.

Despite the sharp decline, some experts argue that it is still too early to dismiss the potential of AI Agents. Guy Turner, founder of Coin Bureau, believes that with the right catalyst, the sector could not only recover but also surge to new heights. He points to retail engagement, regulatory clarity, and institutional investment as key growth drivers. Turner suggests that support from governments, tech firms, and financial institutions could legitimize the sector, shifting it from speculation to a major market force. He also acknowledges the possibility of a meme coin resurgence acting as a short-term catalyst, noting that while AI Agent tokens are sometimes dismissed as “meme coins with a chatbot attached,” this perception oversimplifies their true potential. Turner emphasizes that AI agents are a disruptive force, and their value is yet to be fully realized by tech companies.

data.title: Gemini Files Confidentially for IPO as US Crypto Regulations Ease
data.description:
Gemini Trust, a prominent cryptocurrency exchange and custodian, has taken a significant step towards going public by confidentially filing for an initial public offering (IPO). This move comes at a time when the regulatory environment for digital assets in the United States is becoming more favorable. The confidential filing allows Gemini to assess market interest and prepare for the public offering without immediate public scrutiny.

The decision to file for an IPO follows the conclusion of a nearly two-year investigation by the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC). This investigation, which incurred significant legal fees for Gemini, had cast a shadow of uncertainty over the company's future. However, in February, Cameron Winklevoss, one of the co-founders of Gemini, announced that the SEC had confirmed it would not recommend any enforcement action against the company. This clearance has opened the path for Gemini's IPO, which could potentially take place later this year.

Gemini's IPO filing also aligns with a recent White House crypto summit attended by industry leaders, including the Winklevoss twins. The summit marked a notable shift in the U.S. government's approach to digital assets, with strong support expressed for the industry. One of the key outcomes of the summit was the decision to establish a national bitcoin reserve, where the government would store confiscated cryptocurrency assets. While some industry leaders viewed this as a step toward broader cryptocurrency adoption, others saw it as largely symbolic.

With regulatory hurdles now cleared, Gemini's IPO has the potential to be one of the largest public offerings in the crypto industry. The exchange operates in multiple global markets, including New York, Singapore, London, and Dublin. In late 2024, Gemini expanded its services to France in anticipation of new EU cryptocurrency regulations. A successful IPO would further solidify Gemini's position as a leading cryptocurrency exchange in an industry that is rapidly growing and evolving.

data.title: Cluster's CLR Token Airdrop Rises 10% of Total Supply
data.description:
As the second week of March unfolds, several crypto airdrops are capturing the attention of market participants. These airdrops present fresh opportunities for investors seeking entry into high-potential projects with minimal or no initial capital. Among the notable airdrops this week are Cluster, Boinkers.io, and w.ai.

Cluster, a cross-chain lending protocol, enables users to bridge, lend, and borrow stablecoins and Liquid Staking Derivatives (LSDs) across multiple blockchains. The project has confirmed an airdrop for its native governance token, CLR. To be eligible for the Cluster airdrop, users must participate in its points campaign, which includes engaging in the invite contest within their Discord server and interacting with the platform’s beta version. The project has allocated 10% of its total CLR supply for airdrops and reserved an additional 18% for liquidity vault incentives. Furthermore, since Cluster integrates LayerZero technology, participants may also be eligible for potential LayerZero (ZRO) airdrops.

Boinkers.io, a popular parody game on Telegram developed by ACID Labs, humorously reflects crypto culture. The game’s native token, BOINK, is already in circulation, and airdrop opportunities for players are emerging. Players can qualify for the Boinkers airdrop by actively engaging with the game on Telegram, collecting in-game items such as the “Blue Toiletbrush” Artifact, and participating in special events designed to reward early adopters with tokens. Previous rewards have included Toncoin (TON) token distributions based on in-game item holdings. Future airdrops could be significant for active players, considering the game’s popularity and the involvement of major investors like a16z Speedrun and NFX. ACID Labs, the developer behind Boinkers.io, recently secured $8 million in funding led by a16z speedrun and NFX. Other participants included fusion VC and prominent gaming executives. This funding will support ACID Labs’ mission to create viral instant games integrated with messaging platforms where people connect and communicate.

w.ai is a decentralized computing project where users can share unused computing power from their devices and earn rewards. The project aims to create a marketplace where users are compensated with “W points” for their contributions. WOMBO Studios and nvidia back the project. data.title: Tesla Backfire Isn't Over: UBS Downgrade and Another Annual Delivery Miss Warning
data.description:
Since Elon Musk took on a senior advisory role in the Trump administration, Wall Street has grown increasingly skeptical of Tesla's growth narrative. ubs, jpmorgan, and goldman sachs have all lowered their target prices, citing concerns over slowing demand, margin pressure, and heightened competition.

On Monday, UBS sharply cut Tesla's Q1 2025 delivery forecast to 367,000 units—a 5% year-over-year decline and a 26% drop from the prior quarter, falling 13% below market expectations. The firm also lowered its full-year forecast to 1.7 million units, marking a 5% decline instead of the previously expected 10% growth.

In response to these headwinds, UBS downgraded Tesla's price target to $225, implying a nearly 30% downside from the last closing price. JPMorgan and Goldman Sachs have also lowered their targets, with JPMorgan setting an even more bearish outlook at $135, valuing tesla at around $400 billion.

Europe and China have emerged as the biggest challenges for Tesla's sales. European deliveries in key markets like Germany, France, Norway, and Spain have plunged by as much as 45% year-over-year, attributed to increasing consumer resistance and growing competition from legacy automakers ramping up EV production.

China's numbers also signal trouble. Wholesale sales in the first two months of 2025 are down 29% year-over-year, with January's retail sales dropping 15%. While Tesla recently introduced a limited version of its Full Self-Driving (FSD) system in China, UBS argues that data-sharing restrictions and regulatory hurdles make it less competitive than its U.S. counterpart.

The delivery slump is directly impacting Tesla's margins. UBS forecasts that Tesla's automotive gross margin (excluding regulatory credits) will fall to 10.3% in Q1 2025, down from 13.6% in Q4 2024 and 16.4% in Q1 2024, missing the market consensus of 13.5%.

To counter slowing demand, Tesla has relied on price cuts and subsidies, further squeezing profitability. UBS is also cautious about Tesla's upcoming low-cost models, warning that while they could boost volumes, they may struggle with cost efficiency, further pressuring margins.

UBS has significantly reduced Tesla's earnings projections, cutting Q1 2025 EPS from $0.52 to $0.37 and full-year EPS from $3.16 to $2.02—a 30% drop, well below the consensus estimate of $2.81.

Tesla's long-term growth story has increasingly shifted toward AI-driven initiatives like Robotaxi and humanoid robots. However, UBS argues that these opportunities are already priced into its lofty valuation. Tesla currently trades at 90 times the consensus 2025 earnings estimate and 129 times UBS's own forecast—far above historical averages.

UBS's $225 price target is based on an 80x P/E ratio for 2026 EPS, which remains elevated. JPMorgan, even more bearish, sees the stock falling to $135, citing a sharp 70% decline in consensus profit forecasts since 2022, despite Tesla's stock price doubling over the same period.

Crypto Market Plummets: Bitcoin Drops 6%, Ethereum Falls 5% Amid Policy Uncertainty
data.description:
The recent downturn in the crypto market has left investors scrambling for answers. The sharp decline can be attributed to a combination of factors, including changes in government policies, regulatory uncertainty, and global economic instability. These elements have collectively contributed to a market meltdown, causing significant losses for major cryptocurrencies.

One of the primary triggers for the market crash has been the introduction of new economic policies. The administration's strategies, which include reciprocal tariffs set to take effect in April, have raised fears of a potential recession. This uncertainty has led to a sell-off in both traditional and digital assets, with the S&P 500 experiencing one of its worst days in months. Bitcoin, in particular, has been heavily impacted, falling more than 6% after a disappointing White House crypto summit. The lack of clear direction from the government has left investors hesitant, causing them to move away from riskier assets like crypto.

Regulatory uncertainty has also played a significant role in the market's decline. The repeal of a law requiring decentralized finance (DeFi) platforms to report transactions to the IRS initially seemed like a positive development for crypto. However, it has only added to the confusion, making investors more cautious. Additionally, the announcement of a U.S. Crypto Strategic Reserve, which could include major cryptocurrencies like Bitcoin, Ethereum, Ripple, Solana, and Cardano, has left many questioning its impact on the market. The lack of details about how this reserve will operate has further exacerbated investor concerns, with some fearing that government intervention could disrupt the decentralized nature of crypto.

Global economic worries have also weighed heavily on the crypto market. Factors such as deflation risks and escalating trade tensions have dampened investor sentiment. Asian markets have suffered, while European markets remain cautiously optimistic. Commodities like crude oil and Bitcoin have declined, reflecting a risk-averse climate. The uncertainty surrounding U.S. policies, coupled with global economic struggles, has made investors nervous about the future of crypto. Many are shifting their funds into stable assets, waiting for clearer signals from the market.

Major cryptocurrencies have reacted differently to these challenges. Bitcoin has fallen below $82,000 due to uncertainty surrounding U.S. government involvement in crypto. Ethereum has dropped by about 5%, struggling to stay above $2,000. XRP has plunged to $2.19, marking

TSMC's Soaring February Shows The 'AI Boom' Is Still Here!
data.description:
On March 10th, tsmc released its February 2025 revenue report. The report revealed that TSMC's February sales reached NT$260.01 billion, setting a new record for the same period in previous years, despite a month-on-month decrease of 11.3%. The year-on-year growth rate accelerated to 43.1%, up from 35.9% in the previous month.

The report also showed that TSMC's cumulative sales up to February amounted to NT$553.30 billion, a year-on-year increase of 39.2%, faster than the same period last year. Analysts generally expect TSMC's revenue to grow by approximately 41% this quarter.

Previously, TSMC issued a statement estimating that the losses caused by the Chiayi earthquake in the first quarter would reach around NT$5.3 billion, with some wafers in production being affected.

Some analysts point out that as major AI companies continue to aggressively expand their infrastructure, TSMC's revenue is expected to maintain rapid growth. It is projected that by 2026, revenue from its N2 node process could reach $30 billion, accounting for 22.5% of total revenue.

Earlier this month, TSMC announced plans to invest an additional 165 billion. Analysts believe, for TSMC, agreeing to produce more chips in the United States could help avoid the potential tariffs of up to 50% that Trump had hinted at.

The threat of tariffs would not only pressure TSMC to share costs with its American customers but, more importantly, could lead to the revocation of an agreement reached during the Biden administration. This agreement involves Washington committing to provide over $6 billion in subsidies to TSMC's U.S. factories.

As a result, since the beginning of this year, TSMC's U.S. stock price has fallen by more than 12%. However, analysts believe that TSMC's advanced technology and strong market position make it a solid long-term investment, and the recent stock price correction may present a buying opportunity.
